title: 1.1: Identifies a resize entry point function in screen.c (e.g., screen_resize).
1.2: Identifies a rewrap function in line-buf.c (e.g., linebuf_rewrap).
1.3: Explains that LineBuf holds visible screen content and HistoryBuf holds scrollback.
1.4: Identifies the sequence: history buffer is rewrapped before the screen buffer.
1.5: Explains that a wrap flag (e.g., next_char_was_wrapped) indicates line continuation.
1.6: Identifies a function in history.c that detects if the last history line has the wrap flag set (e.g., history_buf_endswith_wrap).
1.8: Explains that the rewrap function signature lacks a parameter for history wrap state.
1.9: Explains that cursor positions are tracked through the rewrap via coordinate parameters.
